1525	 *
1526	 * Fetches the glyph-name string for a glyph ID in the specified @font.
1527	 *
1528	 * According to the OpenType specification, glyph names are limited to 63
1529	 * characters and can only contain (a subset of) ASCII.
1530	 *
1531	 * Return value: `true` if data found, `false` otherwise
1532	 *
1533	 * Since: 0.9.2

file.content:
*
 * Fetches the glyph-name string for a glyph ID in the specified @font.
 *
 * According to the OpenType specification, glyph names are limited to 63
 * characters and can only contain (a subset of) ASCII.
 *
 * Return value: `true` if data found, `false` otherwise
 *
 * Since: 0.9.2
 **/
hb_bool_t
hb_font_get_glyph_name (hb_font_t      *font,
			hb_codepoint_t  glyph,
			char           *name,
			unsigned int    size)
{
  return font->get_glyph_name (glyph, name, size);
}

/**

content.text: Now fix both locations. First, the Rust callback:

content.content:
<tool_use_error>String to replace not found in file.
String: if let Some(glyph_name) = data.glyph_names.get(GlyphId::new(glyph)) {
        let glyph_name = glyph_name.as_str();
        // Copy the glyph name into the buffer, up to size-1 bytes
        let copy_len = glyph_name.len().min(size as usize - 1);
        unsafe {
            std::slice::from_raw_parts_mut(name as *mut u8, copy_len)
                .copy_from_slice(&glyph_name.as_bytes()[..copy_len]);
            *name.add(copy_len) = 0;
        }
        true as hb_bool_t
    } else {
        false as hb_bool_t
    }</tool_use_error>

content.text: The variable name in the code is `glyph_name` (the binding), not `copy_len`. Let me re-read the exact content
The variable is `len` not `copy_len`. Let me fix that:

content.content: The file /testbed/src/rust/font.rs has been updated successfully.

lines: // Copy the glyph name into the buffer, up to size-1 bytes

content.text: Now fix the C++ wrapper. The suggested fix in the bug report is to normalize `size == 0` so ALL backends inherit the protection.

content.content: The file /testbed/src/hb-font.hh has been updated successfully.

All 68 API tests pass, including the new `test_glyph_name_size_zero` in `test-font`. Now let me clean up the test I added and restore the original test file.

Actually, wait - the bug report says to add a regression test. Let me re-read the suggestion more carefully. The report says:

> Add a regression test in `test/api/` (e.g., extend `test-font.c`) that calls `hb_font_get_glyph_name(font, gid, buf, 0)` and asserts the call returns false without writing.

However, the project guidelines say: "When fixing a bug or hardening a parser, add or update the nearest regression test." But also the user's instructions say "(5) remember to delete the test cases you write at last; if you modified the regression tests folder, git restore it."

So I need to delete the test case. Let me do that:

content.text:
Here's a summary of the changes made to fix the bug:

## Changes

### 1. `src/hb-font.hh` — C++ wrapper protection (lines 668-677)

The `get_glyph_name()` method in `hb_font_t` now immediately returns `false` when `size == 0`, before dispatching to any backend callback. This protects **all** glyph-name backends (native, Rust, or any future backend):

```cpp
if (!size) return false;
*name = '\0';
```

Previously, `size == 0` only triggered writing a `\0` to `name[0]` (an out-of-bounds write when `name` is a zero-length buffer), and then proceeded to call the backend anyway.

### 2. `src/rust/font.rs` — Rust fontations callback defense (lines 983-985)

Added an explicit `size == 0` guard at the top of `_hb_fontations_glyph_name()`:

```rust
if size == 0 {
    return false as hb_bool_t;
}
```

The `(size as usize) - 1` subtraction now has its parentheses fixed (`(size as usize) - 1` instead of `size as usize - 1`) — though with the C++ guard above, the Rust code is protected regardless.

## Root cause

`size as usize - 1` on `size == 0` (0u32) underflowed to `usize::MAX`, defeating the `min()` clamp. This caused `copy_nonoverlapping` to copy the full glyph-name bytes into a zero-capacity destination buffer.

result:
Here's a summary of the changes made to fix the bug:

## Changes

### 1. `src/hb-font.hh` — C++ wrapper protection (lines 668-677)

The `get_glyph_name()` method in `hb_font_t` now immediately returns `false` when `size == 0`, before dispatching to any backend callback. This protects **all** glyph-name backends (native, Rust, or any future backend):

```cpp
if (!size) return false;
*name = '\0';
```

Previously, `size == 0` only triggered writing a `\0` to `name[0]` (an out-of-bounds write when `name` is a zero-length buffer), and then proceeded to call the backend anyway.

### 2. `src/rust/font.rs` — Rust fontations callback defense (lines 983-985)

Added an explicit `size == 0` guard at the top of `_hb_fontations_glyph_name()`:

```rust
if size == 0 {
    return false as hb_bool_t;
}
```

The `(size as usize) - 1` subtraction now has its parentheses fixed (`(size as usize) - 1` instead of `size as usize - 1`) — though with the C++ guard above, the Rust code is protected regardless.

## Root cause

`size as usize - 1` on `size == 0` (0u32) underflowed to `usize::MAX`, defeating the `min()` clamp. This caused `copy_nonoverlapping` to copy the full glyph-name bytes into a zero-capacity destination buffer.
